deep_dive.title: PatternGSL: A Structured Specification Language for Template-Free and Simulation-Ready 3D Garments
deep_dive.abstract: Reconstructing realistic, physically plausible garments from a single image remains a fundamental challenge. Template-free methods capture surface geometry but lack explicit sewing structure for simulation; while programmatic systems are simulation-ready but constrained by predefined templates. This reveals a fundamental representation gap between geometric reconstruction and structured garment construction. We present PatternGSL, a structured garment representation in the form of a template-free and learnable specification language that encodes complete sewing patterns, including panel boundaries, parameterized seams, and explicit stitch topology, in a compact and standardized form. PatternGSL preserves the physical rigor of pattern-based models while removing template dependence, elevating sewing structure as a first-class target for generative modeling. We further propose a vision-language framework that predicts PatternGSL specifications directly from a single image and decodes them into garments using lightweight deterministic validity handling, without optimization-based refinement or manual cleanup. In addition, we introduce PatternGSLData, the first large-scale image-to-GSL paired dataset comprising 300K samples with complete sewing pattern annotations, enabling supervised VLM training for structured garment reconstruction. Experiments demonstrate improved pattern accuracy over prior baselines, explicit sewing-structure recovery, reliable cloth simulation, and pattern-level editing through the same deterministic decoding pipeline. Code and data-processing scripts will be released at https://github.com/PatternGSL/PatternGSL.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: reconstructs 3D garments from a single image with a learnable specification language
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 从单张图像重建3D服装的可学习规格语言
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 从单张图像重建3D服装的可学习规格语言
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 从单张图像重建3D服装的可学习规格语言
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PatternGSL presents a template-free, learnable specification language for reconstructing simulation-ready 3D garments from a single image, encoding sewing patterns and stitch topology explicitly.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 从单张图像重建可用于物理模拟的3D服装是一个挑战。现有无模板方法缺乏缝纫结构，而程序化方法受限于模板。本文提出PatternGSL，一种可学习的结构化规格语言，直接编码完整的缝纫图案和缝合拓扑，实现了无模板且可模拟的3D服装重建。实验证明该方法能生成物理真实的服装模型，弥合了几何重建与结构构造之间的鸿沟。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 从单张图像重建可用于物理模拟的3D服装是一个挑战。现有无模板方法缺乏缝纫结构，而程序化方法受限于模板。本文提出PatternGSL，一种可学习的结构化规格语言，直接编码完整的缝纫图案和缝合拓扑，实现了无模板且可模拟的3D服装重建。实验证明该方法能生成物理真实的服装模型，弥合了几何重建与结构构造之间的鸿沟。

deep_dive.title: PatternGSL: A Structured Specification Language for Template-Free and Simulation-Ready 3D Garments
deep_dive.abstract: Reconstructing realistic, physically plausible garments from a single image remains a fundamental challenge. Template-free methods capture surface geometry but lack explicit sewing structure for simulation; while programmatic systems are simulation-ready but constrained by predefined templates. This reveals a fundamental representation gap between geometric reconstruction and structured garment construction. We present PatternGSL, a structured garment representation in the form of a template-free and learnable specification language that encodes complete sewing patterns, including panel boundaries, parameterized seams, and explicit stitch topology, in a compact and standardized form. deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: single image 3D garment reconstruction with structured pattern language
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 单图3D服装重建，使用结构化版型语言
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 单图3D服装重建，使用结构化版型语言
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 单图3D服装重建，使用结构化版型语言
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PatternGSL proposes a template-free specification language encoding sewing patterns for simulation-ready 3D garment reconstruction from a single image, bridging geometric and structured representations.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 从单张图像重建可仿真的3D服装面临几何重建与结构化版型之间的鸿沟。PatternGSL提出一种无模板的可学习规范语言，编码完整服装版型，包括面板边界、参数化缝线和缝合拓扑。该方法在保持物理真实性的同时支持任意服装样式，实验表明生成结果可直接用于布料仿真。该工作为单图3D服装重建提供了新的结构化表示范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 从单张图像重建可仿真的3D服装面临几何重建与结构化版型之间的鸿沟。PatternGSL提出一种无模板的可学习规范语言，编码完整服装版型，包括面板边界、参数化缝线和缝合拓扑。该方法在保持物理真实性的同时支持任意服装样式，实验表明生成结果可直接用于布料仿真。该工作为单图3D服装重建提供了新的结构化表示范式。

deep_dive.title: Extracting Neural Materials from Multi-view Images
deep_dive.abstract: Neural materials can represent complex specular reflections and scattering effects in a compact, universal basis. However, acquiring and authoring such materials remains challenging. We present NeuMatEx, a differentiable inverse rendering method for extracting spatially varying neural materials from images. The nonlinear structure of neural material latent spaces makes optimization with naive inverse rendering infeasible. To address this, we train a Large Material Reconstruction Model (LMRM) that directly predicts initialbase color, neural material latents, and aleatoric uncertainty guides from images. This material prior provides a good initialization and better constrains our subsequent optimization using inverse path tracing. The predicted uncertainty further helps by anchoring high-confidence regions more tightly to the LMRM prediction, preventing lighting and complex specular effects from being baked into materials. Experiments on synthetic and real assets show that NeuMatEx extracts complex materials with better visual quality and material decomposition than PBR-based methods.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: differentiable inverse rendering for neural material extraction with a large reconstruction model
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 可微逆渲染提取神经材质，结合大材质重建模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 可微逆渲染提取神经材质，结合大材质重建模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 可微逆渲染提取神经材质，结合大材质重建模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: NeuMatEx extracts neural materials from multi-view images via a Large Material Reconstruction Model providing initialization, followed by inverse path tracing optimization.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 为克服神经材质隐空间优化困难，NeuMatEx先训练一个大材质重建模型(LMRM)从图像直接预测初始材质参数，再利用逆路径追踪进行精细化优化。该方法在真实数据集上展示了高质量材质重建结果，能够捕捉复杂的反射和散射效果，对神经渲染和3D内容创作具有实用价值。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 为克服神经材质隐空间优化困难，NeuMatEx先训练一个大材质重建模型(LMRM)从图像直接预测初始材质参数，再利用逆路径追踪进行精细化优化。该方法在真实数据集上展示了高质量材质重建结果，能够捕捉复杂的反射和散射效果，对神经渲染和3D内容创作具有实用价值。
